title: Practitioner's Playbook - AI Discovery Review Workflow
subject: Do this today: Cut your next discovery review time in half (step-by-step)
preheader: The exact workflow a 50-person firm uses to review 100K documents in 3 days
bodyHtml:
LegalTech AI Hub

The Practitioner’s Playbook

Tactical guides you can use this week

The AI-Assisted Discovery Review Workflow That Actually Works

TIME: 1 HOUR SETUP · DIFFICULTY: INTERMEDIATE · SAVES: 40-60% REVIEW TIME

A litigation partner at a 50-attorney firm shared this workflow with us. They used it to review 100,000 documents in 3 days with a team of 4 people. The same review would have taken 2 weeks with traditional methods. Here’s exactly how they did it.

Before you start:

• You need an AI-assisted review platform (Relativity with AI, Everlaw, Disco, or similar)
• A clear set of review issues/categories defined before you begin
• At least one reviewer who understands both the case and the tool
• A seed set of 200-500 manually reviewed documents

Phase 1: Seed Set Training (Day 1, Morning)

Start with your best reviewer — the person who knows the case cold. Have them manually review 200-500 documents, coding each for relevance and issue tags. This is the most important step. Garbage seed set = garbage AI predictions. Spend the time here.

Key tip: Include both clear positives, clear negatives, AND borderline documents in your seed set. The AI needs to learn the boundaries, not just the extremes.

Phase 2: AI-Assisted Prioritization (Day 1, Afternoon)

Run the AI model on the full document set. It will score every document for likely relevance. Do not auto-code based on these scores. Instead, sort documents into three buckets:

High confidence relevant (score >0.85): Still needs human review, but prioritize these first
Medium confidence (score 0.40-0.85): This is where human judgment matters most. Review these second
Low confidence irrelevant (score <0.40): Sample-check these; don’t skip them entirely

Phase 3: Human Review With AI Assist (Days 2-3)

Now your team reviews, starting with the high-confidence bucket. As they review, two things happen simultaneously:

1. The AI model retrains continuously. Every human coding decision improves the model. By the end of Day 2, the AI’s predictions for the remaining documents are significantly better than they were on Day 1.

2. Reviewers move faster. The AI pre-populates suggested codes. Reviewers aren’t starting from scratch on each document — they’re confirming or correcting. This alone cuts review time by 30-40%.

Phase 4: Quality Control (End of Day 3)

Pull a random sample of 200 documents from each bucket. Have your senior reviewer check them. Calculate your recall and precision rates. If they’re below your threshold (typically 80%+ recall for relevance), identify the error patterns and adjust.

For the low-confidence bucket, sample at a higher rate (10-15%). This is where the AI is most likely to have missed relevant documents.

THE RESULTS

The firm that shared this workflow reported: 100K documents reviewed in 3 days (vs. 14 days estimated without AI), 92% recall rate on QC sampling, and a 55% cost reduction compared to their previous review of a similarly-sized matter. The key: they didn’t try to remove humans from the process. They used AI to make humans faster and more focused.

Want to compare e-discovery tools? See our side-by-side reviews. And use our Doc Diff tool for comparing document versions that come up during review.

Try this on your next review and reply with how it went. We’re collecting data on real-world results.
